data.narrative:
Neros is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Soren Monroe-Anderson and Olaf Hichwa, which has raised $121M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparables: 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, plus 2 public market proxies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Key private peers include Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion, valued between $300M and $1.5B at Series B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $121M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $230M. Peer-median analysis across the 8 same-stage, same-sector private comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with the peer set ranging from $300M to $1.5B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $195M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $677M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects material uncertainty around whether Neros should anchor to capital-raised progression or to the maturity and traction demonstrated by existing Series B peers in defense tech. Key uncertainties include product differentiation, customer concentration, and revenue scale relative to the peer cohort.
